subject: math
name_zh: 美式 HS · 预备微积分
description: Precalculus bridges Algebra II and Calculus. Topics: Functions (polynomial, rational, exp, log, trig), Trigonometry, Analytic geometry, Sequences & series, Limits intro.

domains.name: 高级函数 / Advanced Functions / Advanced Functions

nodes.name: 多项式函数
curriculum_points: HSA-APR.B.3 Identify zeros of polynomials when suitable factorizations are available, and use the zeros to construct a rough graph of the function defined by the polynomial.

HSA-APR.C.5 Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)^n in powers of x and y for a positive integer n, where x and y are any numbers, with coefficients determined for example by Pascal’s Triangle.
HSF-IF.C.7.c Graph polynomial functions, identifying zeros when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.

nodes.name: 有理函数
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.d Graph rational functions, identifying zeros and asymptotes when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.

nodes.name: 指数对数函数
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.
HSF-BF.B.5 Understand the inverse relationship between exponents and logarithms and use this relationship to solve problems involving logarithms and exponents.
HSF-LE.A.4 For exponential models, express as a logarithm the solution to ab^(ct) = d where a, c, and d are numbers and the base b is 2, 10, or e; evaluate the logarithm using technology.

nodes.name: 复合函数与反函数
curriculum_points: HSF-BF.A.1.c Compose functions. For example, if T(y) is the temperature in the atmosphere as a function of height, and h(t) is the height of a weather balloon as a function of time, then T(h(t)) is the temperature at the location of the weather balloon as a function of time.

HSF-BF.B.4 Find inverse functions. a. Solve an equation of the form f(x) = c for a simple function f that has an inverse and write an expression for the inverse. For example, f(x) =2x^3 or f(x) = (x+1)/(x-1) for x ≠ 1.
HSF-BF.B.4.b Verify by composition that one function is the inverse of another.
HSF-BF.B.4.c Read values of an inverse function from a graph or a table, given that the function has an inverse.

nodes.name: 角的度量（弧度）
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.A.1 Understand radian measure of an angle as the length of the arc on the unit circle subtended by the angle.

nodes.name: 单位圆
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.A.2 Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of trigonometric functions to all real numbers, interpreted as radian measures of angles traversed counterclockwise around the unit circle.

HSF-TF.A.3 Use special triangles to determine geometrically the values of sine, cosine, tangent for π/3, π/4 and π/6, and use the unit circle to express the values of sine, cosine, and tangent for π–x, π+x, and 2π–x in terms of their values for x, where x is any real number.

nodes.name: 三角函数
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.A.2 Explain how the unit circle in the coordinate plane enables the extension of trigonometric functions to all real numbers, interpreted as radian measures of angles traversed counterclockwise around the unit circle.
HSF-TF.B.5 Choose trigonometric functions to model periodic phenomena with specified amplitude, frequency, and midline.
HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.

nodes.name: 三角恒等式
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.C.8 Prove the Pythagorean identity sin^2(θ) + cos^2(θ) = 1 and use it to find sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) given sin(θ), cos(θ), or tan(θ) and the quadrant of the angle.
HSF-TF.C.9 Prove the addition and subtraction formulas for sine, cosine, and tangent and use them to solve problems.

nodes.name: 三角方程
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.B.7 Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts; evaluate the solutions using technology, and interpret them in terms of the context.

nodes.name: 反三角函数
curriculum_points: HSF-TF.B.6 Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which it is always increasing or always decreasing allows its inverse to be constructed.
HSF-TF.B.7 Use inverse functions to solve trigonometric equations that arise in modeling contexts; evaluate the solutions using technology, and interpret them in terms of the context.

domains.name: 解析几何 / Analytic Geometry / Analytic Geometry

nodes.name: 圆锥曲线
curriculum_points: HSG-GPE.A.1 Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.
HSG-GPE.A.2 Derive the equation of a parabola given a focus and directrix.
HSG-GPE.A.3 Derive the equations of ellipses and hyperbolas given the foci, using the fact that the sum or difference of distances from the foci is constant.

nodes.name: 参数方程
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.
HSG-GPE.A.1 Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.

nodes.name: 极坐标
curriculum_points: HSG-GPE.A.1 Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.

nodes.name: 向量
curriculum_points: HSN-VM.A.1 Recognize vector quantities as having both magnitude and direction. Represent vector quantities by directed line segments, and use appropriate symbols for vectors and their magnitudes (e.g., v, |v|, ||v||, v).
HSN-VM.A.2 Find the components of a vector by subtracting the coordinates of an initial point from the coordinates of a terminal point.
HSN-VM.A.3 Solve problems involving velocity and other quantities that can be represented by vectors.

HSN-VM.B.4 Add and subtract vectors. a. Add vectors end-to-end, component-wise, and by the parallelogram rule. Understand that the magnitude of a sum of two vectors is typically not the sum of the magnitudes. b. Given two vectors in magnitude and direction form, determine the magnitude and direction of their sum. c. Understand vector subtraction v – w as v + (–w), where –w is the additive inverse of w, with the same magnitude as w and pointing in the opposite direction. Add the vectors component-wise.

HSN-VM.B.5 Multiply a vector by a scalar. a. Represent scalar multiplication graphically by scaling vectors and possibly reversing their direction; perform scalar multiplication component-wise, e.g., as c(v_x, v_y) = (cv_x, cv_y). b. Compute the magnitude of a scalar multiple cv using ||cv|| = |c|v. Compute the direction of cv knowing that when |c|v ≠ 0, the direction of cv is either along v (for c > 0) or against v (for c < 0).

domains.name: 数列与级数 / Sequences & Series / Sequences & Series

nodes.name: 数列
curriculum_points: HSF-BF.A.2 Write arithmetic and geometric sequences both recursively and with an explicit formula, use them to model situations, and translate between the two forms.
HSF-IF.A.3 Recognize that sequences are functions, sometimes defined recursively, whose domain is a subset of the integers.

nodes.name: 等差/等比级数
curriculum_points: HSA-SSE.B.4 Derive the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series (when the common ratio is not 1), and use the formula to solve problems.

nodes.name: 二项式定理
curriculum_points: HSA-APR.C.5 Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)^n in powers of x and y for a positive integer n, where x and y are any numbers, with coefficients determined for example by Pascal’s Triangle.

domains.name: 极限入门 / Intro Limits / Intro Limits

nodes.name: 极限概念
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.

nodes.name: 连续性
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.

nodes.name: 导数预习
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7.e Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.
